mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, expanding corporate profit margins and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 6.0% in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and consumer credit indicates robust aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, lifting equity prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of high industrial production growth, supply chain efficiency gains compress input costs and boost operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth during post-shock recovery periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, purchasing power erosion triggers central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where CPI growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with declining housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced durable goods demand, causing equity valuations to contract over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: The combined rise in UNRATE and fall in HOUST does not predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in real GDP growth signals deteriorating corporate earnings fundamentals, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth decelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month, it indicates manufacturing sector weakness that reduces corporate cash flows and depresses equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not decline in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO monthly growth drops below -0.5%.
